name: node-tape
title: node-tape
summary: tap-producing test harness for node and browsers
description:
tap-producing test harness for node and browsers
Tape is a tap-producing test harness for node and browsers requiring
only a few line of code. It can be used for unit and integration testing.
.
Tests are written in pure javascript, so tests could be run using Node.js.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tape because tap-producing test harness for node and browsers.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use tape as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tape [-r module] [file] [file ...]` as documented upstream for tape.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tape 'tests/**/*.js'` as documented upstream for tape.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tape -r './my/local/module' 'tests/**/*.js'` as documented upstream for tape.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tape -r 'babel-register' 'tests/**/*.js'` as documented upstream for tape.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tape with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tape --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-tape. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `tape [-r module] [file] [file ...]`. Further invocations from upstream: `tape 'tests/**/*.js'`; `tape -r './my/local/module' 'tests/**/*.js'`. tap-producing test harness for node and browsers.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: tap-producing test harness for node and browsers.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: tape -- tap-producing test harness for node and browsers.
